name: Web Identity Conformance Demo
summary: The runnable proof of the CustomCredentialProvider contract (#1060) in a real browser: supports(family, member) gates what a provider serves and acquire(request) resolves to a CredentialResult (fulfilled / declined / unavailable) across passkey / federated / digital / password families. The contract is type-only (identity/contract.ts); the real passkey / FedCM / digital-credential providers (RP/IdP round-trips) are impl in Frontier UI, so the demo supplies its own in-demo capability-gated provider. A runtime-conformance section asserts each invariant live: supports() gates by family+member, acquire() fulfills a supported family, reports unavailable for an unsupported one, declines under silent mediation, names the resolved family, and subscribe() returns a revocable cleanup.
description:
Concept

The runnable proof of the CustomCredentialProvider contract (#1060). supports(family, member) gates what a provider serves and acquire(request) resolves to a CredentialResult (fulfilled/declined/unavailable).

In-demo provider, not the FUI impl

The contract is type-only (identity/contract.ts); the real passkey / FedCM / digital-credential providers (RP/IdP round-trips) are impl in Frontier UI. So this demo supplies its own capability-gated in-demo provider — honest for a browser demo with no RP — to prove the contract is realizable.

Verified contract

The runtime-conformance section asserts each invariant live: supports() gates by family+member, acquire() fulfills a supported family, reports unavailable for an unsupported family (never a silent substitute), declines under silent mediation, names the resolved family, and subscribe() returns a revocable cleanup.
